 {"id":"bd-05a8","title":"Split large cmd/bd files: doctor.go (2948 lines), sync.go (2121 lines)","description":"Code health review found several oversized files:

1. doctor.go - 2948 lines, 48 functions mixed together
   - Should split into doctor/checks/*.go for individual diagnostics
   - applyFixes() and previewFixes() are nearly identical

2. sync.go - 2121 lines
   - ZFC (Zero Flush Check) logic embedded inline (lines 213-247)
   - Multiple mode handlers should be extracted

3. init.go - 1732 lines
4. compact.go - 1097 lines
5. show.go - 1069 lines

Recommendation: Extract into focused sub-packages or split into logical files.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:18.169927-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T23:58:24.591999-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T23:58:24.591999-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-077e","title":"Add close_reason field to CLI schema and documentation","description":"PR #551 persists close_reason, but the CLI documentation may not mention this field as part of the issue schema.

## Current State
- close_reason is now persisted in database
- `bd show --json` will return close_reason in JSON output
- Documentation may not reflect this new field

## What's Missing
- CLI reference documentation for close_reason field
- Schema documentation showing close_reason is a top-level issue field
- Example output showing close_reason in bd show --json
- bd close command documentation should mention close_reason parameter is optional

## Suggested Action
1. Update README.md or CLI reference docs to list close_reason as an issue field
2. Add example to bd close documentation
3. Update any type definitions or schema specs
4. Consider adding close_reason to verbose list output (bd list --verbose)","status":"hooked","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:28.448654-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.350245-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-077e","depends_on_id":"bd-z86n","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:28.449968-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-0a43","title":"Split monolithic sqlite.go into focused files","description":"internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go is 1050 lines containing initialization, 20+ CRUD methods, query building, and schema management.

Split into:
- store.go: Store struct & initialization (150 lines)
- bead_queries.go: Bead CRUD (300 lines)
- work_queries.go: Work queries (200 lines)  
- stats_queries.go: Statistics (150 lines)
- schema.go: Schema & migrations (150 lines)
- helpers.go: Common utilities (100 lines)

Impact: Impossible to understand at a glance; hard to find specific functionality; high cognitive load

Effort: 6-8 hours","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:16.520465-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-0d5p","title":"Fix TestRunSync_Timeout failing on macOS","description":"The hooks timeout test fails because exec.CommandContext doesn't properly terminate child processes of shell scripts on macOS. The test creates a hook that runs 'sleep 60' with a 500ms timeout, but it waits the full 60 seconds.

Options to fix:
- Use SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true} to create process group and kill the group
- Skip test on darwin with build tag
- Use a different approach for timeout testing

Location: internal/hooks/hooks_test.go:220-253","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T20:52:51.771217-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-0e02","title":"Code smell: Inconsistent error handling patterns","description":"Commands use a mix of error handling patterns:
- FatalError()
- FatalErrorRespectJSON()
- fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) + os.Exit(1)
- Silent continue
- Inconsistent resource cleanup (result.Close())

**Example in show.go lines 89-146:**
Different error paths use different patterns, some close resources, some don't.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Document error handling contract
- [ ] Standardize on consistent pattern
- [ ] Use defer for resource cleanup
- [ ] Create helper for common fetch-and-handle pattern
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T18:59:30.118-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.362524-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:02:03.530317-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: Added LogError, LogErrorRespectJSON, LogNotFound, LogNotFoundRespectJSON helpers to errors.go. Standardized show.go to use these helpers. Updated ERROR_HANDLING.md documentation. Tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0e02","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.683836-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ta4r","title":"Wisp operations should auto-bypass daemon","description":"During the v0.39.1 release, every wisp operation required --no-daemon flag:

bd --no-daemon mol wisp create beads-release --var version=0.39.1
bd --no-daemon close bd-eph-xxx
bd --no-daemon mol burn bd-eph-bv2

Since wisps are ephemeral (Ephemeral=true) and never exported to JSONL, they are inherently local-only. The daemon cannot help with them anyway.

Proposal: When operating on bd-eph-* issues or wisp subcommands, auto-detect and bypass the daemon. This would:
- Reduce friction in wisp workflows
- Prevent accidental daemon use that would fail anyway
- Make the ephemeral nature more obvious","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-27T22:51:24.409066-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-28T02:10:30.123755-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T02:10:30.123755-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-tbz3","title":"bd init UX Improvements","description":"bd init leaves users with incomplete setup, requiring manual bd doctor --fix. Issues found: (1) git hooks not installed if user declines prompt, (2) no auto-migration when CLI is upgraded, (3) stale merge driver configs from old versions. Fix by making bd init more robust with better defaults and auto-migration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-21T23:16:00.333543-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:20:51.88847-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:20:51.88847-08:00"}
